rationale: Strong first-pass amorphous-stabilization route for neutral aggregation-prone APIs.
Cellulose-ether route with a volatile co-solvent profile that is useful for print-focused semi-solids.
Simple aqueous polyether vehicle for baseline comparison against stronger polymer binders.
Proteinaceous hydrogen-bonding comparator when the API has multiple donor and acceptor sites.
Cellulose-ether aqueous route that usually tolerates polar APIs while preserving a printable semi-solid path.
PVP still acts as a strong local binder for polar neutrals and is worth keeping in the early shortlist.
Polysaccharide gel-network comparator for very water-affine APIs.
Cellulose-ether route is a strong first screen for acidic or anionic APIs in printable semi-solid contexts.
PVP remains a useful comparator for acidic APIs because it can still offer strong local H-bonding and dispersion support.
An anionic polysaccharide gel route is worth screening when the goal includes robust gel-network behavior.
Pectin plus glycerol-water is a softer gel-route comparator for acidic APIs and drying-sensitive systems.
Keep a simple vehicle-like control to separate polymer-binding gains from solvent-only effects.
PVP is a strong first route for cationic APIs because it often balances local stabilization with simple aqueous processing.
Cellulose-ether plus volatile co-solvent remains a good print-focused alternative for cationic APIs.
Oppositely charged or highly polar gel-network routes can be informative when ionic reorganization matters.
Polysaccharide gel comparator for cationic APIs where local electrostatic reorganization may matter.
Amphiphilic APIs often benefit from a balanced polar-apolar local environment and strong local binder contacts.
Cellulose-ether plus ethanol-water covers a useful middle ground when both polarity and drying behavior matter.
Flexible solvent-rich comparator for amphiphilic APIs.
Flexible polar APIs often sit well in cellulose-ether routes with a controlled volatile co-solvent component.
PVP remains a strong dispersion and local-contact comparator for flexible polar molecules.
Hydrogen-bond-rich peptide-like polymer comparator for flexible polar APIs.
Simple vehicle-like comparator for flexible polar APIs.
PVP is prioritized when aromatic self-association risk is high and local API-polymer binding needs to be tested early.
Cellulose-ether route gives a strong secondary check against aromatic aggregation in a print-relevant solvent setting.
Simple polyether comparator to determine whether stronger binders are truly needed.
Low-complexity aqueous vehicle comparator for broadly hydrophilic neutral APIs.
General fallback route for neutral APIs where API-polymer stabilization is the main concern.
General fallback cellulose-ether route for acidic or anionic APIs.
General fallback PVP route for basic or cationic APIs.
